sentences.base_language: Tariq had been looking forward to the weekend hike for months and woke up early on Saturday morning feeling energised.
sentences.target_language: كان طارق يتطلَّع إلى نزهة عطلة نهاية الأسبوع منذ أشهرٍ، واستيقظ في صباح السبت مبكِّراً وهو يشعر بالنشاط والحيوية.
sentences.romanized: Kāna Ṭāriqun yataṭallaʿu ilā nuzhat ʿuṭlati nihāyati l-usbūʿi mundhu ashurin, wa-stayqaẓa fī ṣabāḥi s-sabti mubakkiran wa-huwa yashʿuru bin-nashāṭi wal-ḥayawiyyah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**كان يتطلَّع** is كان + مضارع, the past progressive/habitual construction expressing an ongoing state in the past — he had been looking forward; يتطلَّع is Form V of طلع (to rise/appear), and the Form V pattern تفعَّل here conveys a reflexive, anticipatory orientation toward something, intensified by the duration marker منذ أشهرٍ.
**وهو يشعر** is a ḥāl clause introduced by wāw al-ḥāl + the pronoun هو, followed by a nominal sentence (هو + imperfect verb); this construction, while he was feeling, attaches a simultaneous circumstance to the main verb استيقظ and is the standard MSA way to express two concurrent actions, one framing the other.

sentences.base_language: He packed his backpack carefully with water, snacks, a rain jacket, and a small first-aid kit.
sentences.target_language: حزَم حقيبةَ ظهره بعنايةٍ، وأودعها الماءَ والوجباتِ الخفيفةَ وسترةَ المطرِ وعلبةَ إسعافٍ أوليةٍ صغيرة.
sentences.romanized: Ḥazama ḥaqībata ẓahrihi bi-ʿināyatin, wa-awdaʿahā l-māʾa wal-wajabāti l-khafīfata wa-sitrata l-maṭari wa-ʿulbata isʿāfin awwaliyyatin ṣaghīrah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أودعها** is Form IV of ودع (أودع, to deposit/entrust), taking two objects — the implicit backpack (ـها) and the list of items in the accusative; Form IV here is causative-ditransitive, meaning to cause something to be lodged inside something, making أودع the precise MSA verb for stowing items in a container.
**حقيبةَ ظهره** is an إضافة chain: حقيبة (bag) governs ظهر (back) in the genitive, which in turn governs the pronoun ـه; the entire chain is in the accusative as the direct object of حزَم, showing the case propagation through a three-element إضافة.

sentences.base_language: The trailhead was about an hour's drive from the city and he arrived just as the sun was appearing over the hills.
sentences.target_language: كانت بداية المسار تبعد نحو ساعةٍ بالسيارة عن المدينة، ووصل إليها لحظةَ أن أخذت الشمسُ تطلع فوق التلال.
sentences.romanized: Kānat bidāyatu l-masāri tabʿudu naḥwa sāʿatin bis-sayyārati ʿani l-madīnati, wa-waṣala ilayhā laḥẓata an akhadhat ash-shamsu taṭluʿu fawqa t-tilāl.
sentences.grammarNote:
**أخذت تطلع** uses the verb أخذ (literally to take) + imperfect as an inceptive construction — began to rise; this أخذ + مضارع pattern, alongside بدأ + مضارع and شرع + في + masdar, is a standard MSA way to express the beginning of an action, with أخذ carrying a nuance of gradual onset.
**لحظةَ أن** is a temporal conjunction meaning at the moment that; لحظة in the accusative functions as a zarf (adverb of time), governing the subordinate clause with أن; this lends the sentence a precision — he arrived at the exact instant — conveying the fortuitous timing.

sentences.base_language: The forest at the start of the trail was cool and peaceful, with birdsong filling the morning air.
sentences.target_language: كانت الغابةُ في مستهلِّ المسار باردةً هادئةً، وكان غناءُ الطيورِ يملأ هواءَ الصباح.
sentences.romanized: Kānat al-ghābatu fī mustahalli l-masāri bāridatan hādiʾatan, wa-kāna ghinā ʾuṭ-ṭuyūri yamlaʾu hawāʾa ṣ-ṣabāḥ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**مستهلِّ** is the noun of place/time on the Form X pattern مُستَفعَل, derived from استهلَّ (to begin, to herald the start of); although Form X is primarily known as the استفعال pattern, مستهلّ functions here as a pure noun — the very beginning — and appears in the genitive as مضاف إليه after في, a formal register term preferred over بداية in literary MSA.
**كان يملأ** is كان + مضارع expressing the past progressive — was filling; غناءُ الطيورِ is an إضافة in the nominative as subject of كان, with the broken plural الطيور (birds, from طائر on the pattern فُعُول) in the genitive, showing both إضافة case government and the irregularity of this common broken plural form.

sentences.base_language: After about thirty minutes of walking, the path became steeper and his breathing grew heavier.
sentences.target_language: بعد نحو ثلاثين دقيقةً من المشي، أصبح المسارُ أكثرَ انحداراً وباتت أنفاسُه أثقل.
sentences.romanized: Baʿda naḥwi thalāthīna daqīqatan min al-mashyi, aṣbaḥa l-masāru akthara inḥidāran wa-bātat anfāsuhu athqal.
literal: بعد نحو ثلاثين دقيقةً من المشي
after about thirty minutes of the-walking
sentences.grammarNote:
**أكثرَ انحداراً** is the comparative superlative construction أكثر + masdar in the accusative as tamyīz; this أكثر + masdar pattern is the standard MSA way to form comparatives from adjectives that do not have a simple أفعل comparative form — انحدار (steepness/gradient, masdar of Form VIII انحدر) is used in the tamyīz position to specify the domain of comparison.
**باتت** is the verb بات (one of the كان sisters), meaning to become/to be at nightfall, used here in a broader sense of to turn into or to have become; like أصبح, it takes an accusative predicate — here أثقل, the elative (comparative) of ثقيل — and its use alongside أصبح in the same sentence adds stylistic variety, both belonging to the same sister-of-كان family.

sentences.base_language: He stopped briefly to drink some water and take in the view of the valley spreading below him.
sentences.target_language: توقَّف قليلاً لِيشرب بعضَ الماء ويستمتع بمنظر الوادي المنبسط تحته.
sentences.romanized: Tawaqkafa qalīlan li-yashraba baʿḍa l-māʾi wa-yastamtiʿa bi-manẓari l-wādī l-munbasiṭi taḥtah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**لِيشرب** uses lām al-taʿlīl (the lām of purpose) + مضارع منصوب (subjunctive); after the lām of purpose, the imperfect verb must be in the subjunctive (مضارع منصوب), shown here by the short final vowel يشرب — so that he might drink; this lām + subjunctive construction is the canonical MSA expression of purpose, more formal than the English to + infinitive.
**المنبسط** is the Form VII active participle (مُنفَعِل) of بسط (انبسط, to spread out/extend); Form VII is the reflexive-intransitive of Form I, and the participle المنبسط describes the valley as spreading out of its own accord, functioning as a post-nominal adjective in the definite genitive agreeing with الوادي.

sentences.base_language: A fellow hiker coming back down warned him there was a slippery section ahead near the stream crossing.
sentences.target_language: حذَّره مُتنزِّهٌ آخر كان عائداً إلى الأسفل من وجود قسمٍ زلِقٍ في الأمام قرب معبر الجدول.
sentences.romanized: Ḥadhdhārahu mutanazzihun ākharu kāna ʿāʾidan ilā l-asfali min wujūdi qismin zaliqin fī l-amāmi qurba maʿbari l-jadwal.
sentences.grammarNote:
**حذَّر** is Form II of حذر (to be cautious), meaning to warn/caution someone; Form II is causative here — to cause someone to be cautious — and takes a double object: the person warned (ـه) and the thing warned about (من وجود...), the latter introduced by من in the standard MSA pattern for warning about something.
**كان عائداً** is كان + active participle, expressing a past progressive state — was in the process of returning; عائد is the Form I active participle of عاد (to return), and the combination كان + participle (rather than كان + imperfect) emphasises the state or posture of the subject at that moment rather than the ongoing action, a subtle distinction in formal Arabic narrative.

sentences.base_language: Tariq thanked them and continued carefully, picking his way across the wet stones with both arms out for balance.
sentences.target_language: شكرهم طارقٌ وتابع السيرَ بحذرٍ، يشقُّ طريقه عبر الحجارة المبتلَّة وذراعاه ممدودتان على الجانبين للحفاظ على توازنه.
sentences.romanized: Shakarahum Ṭāriqun wa-tābaʿa s-sayra bi-ḥadharin, yashiqqu ṭarīqahu ʿabra l-ḥijārati l-mubtalati wa-dhirāʿāhu mamdūdatāni ʿalā l-jānibayni lil-ḥifāẓi ʿalā tawāzunih.
sentences.grammarNote:
**وذراعاه ممدودتان** is a ḥāl clause in the form of a nominal sentence: the dual subject ذراعاه (his two arms, dual nominative) + the dual predicate ممدودتان (extended, dual feminine Form I passive participle in the nominative); nominal ḥāl clauses introduced by wāw al-ḥāl describe a simultaneous background condition, and the dual agreement throughout (ذراعاه / ممدودتان / الجانبين) demonstrates the full Arabic dual paradigm.
**للحفاظ على** uses lām al-taʿlīl + the masdar الحفاظ (Form I verbal noun of حافظ, to preserve/maintain) in إضافة with على and the following noun; this lām + masdar construction expresses purpose in a more nominal, formal register than lām + subjunctive, and الحفاظ على is the standard MSA collocation for maintaining or preserving something.

sentences.base_language: The middle section of the trail was the hardest — a long exposed ridge where the wind was cold and relentless.
sentences.target_language: كان القسمُ الأوسط من المسار هو الأشدَّ وطأةً — نتوءٌ طويلٌ مكشوفٌ حيث كانت الريحُ باردةً لا تهدأ.
sentences.romanized: Kāna l-qismu l-awsaṭu min al-masāri huwa l-ashadda waṭʾatan — natuʾun ṭawīlun makshūfun ḥaythu kānat ar-rīḥu bāridatan lā tahda.
literal: حيث كانت الريحُ باردةً لا تهدأ
where was the-wind cold not it-abates
sentences.grammarNote:
**الأشدَّ وطأةً** is an elative (أفعل التفضيل) in the accusative followed by a tamyīz: الأشد (most intense/hardest, from شديد) + وطأةً (severity/burden) in the indefinite accusative as tamyīz specifying the domain; this أفعل + tamyīz construction is the standard MSA superlative with abstract nouns that lack a direct أفعل form of their own.
**لا تهدأ** is a negative imperfect in an asyndetic relative clause modifying الريح — a wind that does not abate; because the antecedent ريحٌ in the appositive is indefinite, the relative clause is introduced without a relative pronoun, and لا + مضارع (present negation) within a past narrative context conveys habitual or characteristic negation — it never abated.

sentences.base_language: By the time he reached the summit, his legs were burning and his shirt was soaked with sweat.
sentences.target_language: حين بلغ القمَّةَ، كانت ساقاه تحترقان وكان قميصُه مبلَّلاً بالعرق تماماً.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna balaghā l-qimmata, kānat sāqāhu taḥtariqāni wa-kāna qamīṣuhu muballalanً bil-ʿarqi tamāmā.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ساقاه تحترقان** shows dual subject-verb agreement: ساقاه is the dual nominative of ساق (leg) with the pronoun suffix ـه, and تحترقان is the Form VIII imperfect dual feminine — they (two) were burning; Form VIII of حرق (احترق, to burn/be on fire) is the reflexive-intransitive form, expressing that the legs were burning of their own accord rather than being burned by an external agent.
**مبلَّلاً** is the Form II passive participle of بلَّل (to soak/drench), in the accusative as predicate of كان; Form II passive participles (مُفعَّل) derived from Form II verbs expressing intensive action (بلَّل = to thoroughly wet) carry the intensification of that form, so مبلَّل means thoroughly soaked rather than merely damp.

sentences.base_language: But the view from the top was worth every hard step — a vast panorama of mountains and valleys stretching to the horizon.
sentences.target_language: غير أن المنظرَ من القمة كان يستحقُّ كلَّ خطوةٍ شاقَّةٍ — بانوراما شاسعةٌ من الجبال والأودية تمتدُّ حتى الأفق.
sentences.romanized: Ghayra anna l-manẓara min al-qimmati kāna yastaḥiqqu kulla khuṭwatin shāqqatin — bānūrāmā shāsiʿatun min al-jibāli wal-awdiyati tamtaddu ḥattā l-ufuq.
sentences.grammarNote:
**يستحقُّ** is Form X of حقَّ (استحقَّ, to deserve/merit); Form X استفعل here expresses the idea of deeming something rightfully due — to consider something as one's right — and كان يستحقُّ is the past habitual/continuous construction emphasising that the view genuinely merited the effort as an enduring truth, not just a momentary feeling.
**حتى الأفق** uses حتى as a spatial preposition meaning up to / as far as; distinct from حتى as a temporal or causal conjunction, this prepositional حتى governing a definite noun is a classical and formal MSA construction for indicating the limit or endpoint of an extension in space, adding a sweeping quality to the description.

sentences.base_language: He sat on a flat rock, ate his packed food, and let the silence and the scenery wash over him.
sentences.target_language: جلس على صخرةٍ مسطَّحةٍ وتناول طعامَه الذي أحضره معه، وترك الصمتَ والمناظرَ الطبيعيةَ تغمره.
sentences.romanized: Jalasa ʿalā ṣakhbatin musaṭṭaḥatin wa-tanāwala ṭaʿāmahu lladhī aḥḍarahu maʿahu, wa-taraka ṣ-ṣamta wal-manāẓira ṭ-ṭabīʿiyyata taghmuruh.
sentences.grammarNote:
**تناول** is Form VI of نال (تناول, to eat/partake of/reach for), conveying a deliberate, extended act of consuming — not merely أكل (to eat) but eating in a measured, appreciative manner; Form VI تفاعل often implies a reciprocal or iterative process, and تناول specifically carries connotations of deliberate, dignified consumption in formal registers.
**ترك... تغمره** uses ترك + accusative object + imperfect verb as an accusative complement, expressing caused permission — he let [them] wash over him; تغمر is the imperfect of غمر (Form I, to immerse/flood), and the construction ترك + noun + imperfect is the standard MSA pattern for let [something] do [something], analogous to a causative complement.

sentences.base_language: Tariq took dozens of photographs, knowing they would never fully capture what he was seeing.
sentences.target_language: التقط طارقٌ عشراتِ الصور، عالماً في قرارة نفسه أنها لن تستطيع أبداً أن تلتقط ما كان يراه على حقيقته.
sentences.romanized: Iltaqaṭa Ṭāriqun ʿasharāti ṣ-ṣuwari, ʿāliman fī qarārati nafsihi annahā lan tastaṭīʿa abadan an taltaqiṭa mā kāna yarāhu ʿalā ḥaqīqatih.
sentences.grammarNote:
**التقط** is Form VIII of لقط (to pick up/capture), and is the standard MSA verb for capturing a photograph — literally to pick up/snatch an image; Form VIII (افتعل) adds a sense of deliberate, careful seizure of a moment, making it more precise than the borrowed verb صوَّر (to photograph).
**عالماً في قرارة نفسه** uses the active participle عالم (Form I, from علم, to know) in the accusative as a ḥāl, modified by the prepositional phrase في قرارة نفسه (in the depths of his soul); this ḥāl participle expresses Tariq's inner state of awareness simultaneously with the main action of photographing, and قرارة النفس is a classical MSA idiom for the innermost recesses of one's consciousness.

sentences.base_language: A couple nearby told him there was a beautiful waterfall only twenty minutes off the main trail.
sentences.target_language: أخبره زوجٌ من المتنزِّهين كانا بالقرب منه بأن ثمَّة شلَّالاً جميلاً يبعد عشرين دقيقةً فقط عن المسار الرئيسي.
sentences.romanized: Akhbarahu zawjun min al-mutanazzihīna kānā bil-qurbi minhu bi-anna thamma shallālan jamīlan yabʿudu ʿishrīna daqīqatan faqaṭ ʿan al-masāri r-raʾīsī.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ثمَّة** is the formal MSA existential adverb, equivalent to there is/are, used in formal and literary registers in preference to the colloquial فيه or the more common هناك; ثمَّة is invariable and always introduces an indefinite subject — here شلَّالاً — which carries the indefinite accusative tanwīn, the standard pattern for existential sentences in MSA.
**كانا** is the dual past tense of كان, with the dual subject suffix ـا agreeing with زوج (a pair); MSA requires the dual verb form كانا when the subject is dual, and this agreement cascades through the sentence — كانا بالقرب marks them as the pair who were nearby, functioning as an adjectival relative clause modifying زوجٌ.

sentences.base_language: He decided it was worth the extra effort and headed down the narrow side path.
sentences.target_language: قرَّر أن الأمر يستحقُّ الجهدَ الإضافي وانطلق نزولاً على المسار الجانبي الضيِّق.
sentences.romanized: Qarrara anna l-amra yastaḥiqqu l-jahda l-iḍāfiyya wa-nṭalaqa nuzūlan ʿalā l-masāri l-jānibiyyi ḍ-ḍayyiq.
sentences.grammarNote:
**قرَّر أن** uses Form II of قرر (to decide/resolve), taking أنَّ + جملة اسمية as its complement; Form II قرَّر (from Form I قرَّ, to settle/be firm) conveys a definitive, deliberate decision — more resolute than the simple اختار — and the أنَّ clause following it functions as the logical object, a standard MSA complement pattern for verbs of cognition and decision.
**انطلق نزولاً** uses the Form VII verb انطلق (to set off/depart, reflexive of Form I طلق) followed by نزولاً as an adverbial accusative of manner (مفعول مطلق in function) — literally he set off in a downward manner; this combination of a motion verb + directional masdar in the accusative is a formal MSA construction for expressing both the motion and its direction simultaneously.

sentences.base_language: The waterfall was spectacular — white water falling into a clear pool surrounded by green moss.
sentences.target_language: كان الشلَّالُ بديعاً — ماءٌ أبيضُ يتساقط في بركةٍ صافيةٍ يحيط بها الطحلبُ الأخضر.
sentences.romanized: Kāna sh-shallālu badīʿan — māʾun abyaḍu yatasāqaṭu fī birkatin ṣāfiyatin yuḥīṭu bihā aṭ-ṭaḥlabu l-akhḍar.
sentences.grammarNote:
**يتساقط** is Form VI of سقط (تساقط, to fall repeatedly/cascade), the pattern of repeated or distributed action; Form VI here (تفاعل) conveys the tumbling, continuous cascading of the water rather than a single fall, making it the precise choice for describing a waterfall in formal MSA rather than the simple Form I يسقط.
**يحيط بها** uses Form IV of حاط (أحاط, to surround/encircle), taking the preposition بـ; Form IV here is causative — to cause an encirclement — and يحيط بـ is the standard MSA collocation for to surround something; the verb follows its subject الطحلب in VSO order, with بها (the pool) as the prepositional object, and the whole clause functions as an asyndetic relative modifying بركة (indefinite head noun).

sentences.base_language: He stood there for a long time, feeling completely at peace with the world.
sentences.target_language: ظلَّ واقفاً هناك وقتاً طويلاً وهو يشعر بسلامٍ تامٍّ مع الكون.
sentences.romanized: Ẓalla wāqifan hunāka waqtan ṭawīlan wa-huwa yashʿuru bi-salāmin tāmmin maʿa l-kawn.
sentences.grammarNote:
**ظلَّ واقفاً** uses ظلَّ (one of the كان sisters, meaning to remain/continue throughout) + active participle as predicate; ظلَّ + participle or ظلَّ + imperfect expresses continuous, uninterrupted persistence of a state — he remained standing — and is the preferred MSA construction for describing a prolonged static state, more precise than the simple بقى + participle.
**وقتاً طويلاً** is an adverbial accusative of duration (ظرف زمان in the accusative) — for a long time; both words carry indefinite accusative tanwīn, and the phrase modifies the entire clause, specifying how long the state of ظلَّ واقفاً persisted; this adverbial accusative of time is a core MSA construction without a preposition, unlike the English for + noun.

sentences.base_language: On the descent, his knees began to ache and he had to slow down on the rocky sections.
sentences.target_language: في أثناء النزول، بدأت ركبتاه تؤلمانه واضطرَّ إلى إبطاء خطاه في الأجزاء الصخرية.
sentences.romanized: Fī athāʾi n-nuzūli, badaʾat rukbatāhu tuʾlimānihi wa-ḍṭurra ilā ibṭāʾi khuṭāhu fī l-ajzāʾi ṣ-ṣakhriyyah.
sentences.grammarNote:
**اضطرَّ** is Form VIII of ضرَّ (اضطرَّ, to be compelled/forced), taking the preposition إلى + masdar as its complement; Form VIII here is reflexive — to find oneself compelled — rather than causative, and اضطرَّ إلى is the standard MSA idiom for to be forced to do something, stronger and more formal than اضطُرَّ إلى using passive voice.
**ركبتاه تؤلمانه** is a dual subject-predicate with full dual agreement: ركبتاه (his two knees, dual nominative with possessive suffix) + تؤلمانه (dual feminine imperfect of Form IV أألم, to cause pain, with the dual suffix ـانِ and the object pronoun ـه); this dual verb agreement — maintaining ـانِ on the imperfect — is obligatory in MSA with dual subjects and distinguishes formal MSA from colloquial Arabic.

sentences.base_language: Back at the trailhead as the sun set, he sat down and removed his hiking boots with relief.
sentences.target_language: حين عاد إلى بداية المسار مع غروب الشمس، جلس وخلع حذاءَه الجبليَّ وهو يشعر بالارتياح.
sentences.romanized: Ḥīna ʿāda ilā bidāyati l-masāri maʿa ghurūbi sh-shamsi, jalasa wa-khalaʿa ḥidhāʾahu l-jabaliyya wa-huwa yashʿuru bil-irtiyāḥ.
sentences.grammarNote:
**مع غروب الشمس** uses مع + masdar in إضافة as a temporal phrase — at the time of the sun's setting / as the sun set; غروب is the Form I masdar of غرب (to set/go west), and this مع + masdar + إضافة construction is the formal MSA equivalent of a temporal as-clause, compressing a whole subordinate clause into a prepositional phrase.
**الارتياح** is the masdar of Form VIII ارتاح (to rest/feel relief, from راح), used here as the object of بـ after the perception verb يشعر; the Form VIII masdar on the pattern افتعال (ارتياح) nominally expresses the internal experience of finding rest, and يشعر بـ + masdar is the standard MSA construction for to feel [an emotion], taking the masdar as a near-complement specifying the content of the feeling.

sentences.base_language: Driving home with tired legs and a calm full heart, he thought he needed to do this far more often.
sentences.target_language: وهو يقود سيارتَه إلى البيت بساقين متعبتين وقلبٍ هادئٍ مليء، خطر بباله أنه يحتاج إلى أن يفعل هذا أكثرَ بكثير.
sentences.romanized: Wa-huwa yaqūdu sayyāratahu ilā l-bayti bi-sāqayni mutaʿibatayni wa-qalbin hādiʾin malīʾin, khaṭara bi-bālihi annahu yaḥtāju ilā an yafʿala hādhā akthara bi-kathīr.
literal: أنه يحتاج إلى أن يفعل هذا
that-he needs to that he-does this
sentences.grammarNote:
**بساقين متعبتين** shows the dual genitive with adjectival agreement: ساقين is the dual genitive/accusative of ساق (leg), and متعبتين is the dual genitive Form IV active participle of أعب (متعِب, tiring/fatigued) in the feminine dual genitive, agreeing in gender, number, and case; the بـ is a ḥāl-introducing preposition — in a state of having two tired legs — a prepositional ḥāl construction common in formal MSA.
**أكثرَ بكثير** is the comparative elative أكثر (more) + بـ + كثير as a reinforcing differential; this أفعل + بـ + كثير construction is the standard MSA way to express far more / much more, with بكثير (by much) functioning as the measure of the comparative difference, equivalent to the English by far or much.
